user_message: Eric Kim at Coho Winery just sent this. Help me draft a response.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-14 09:17 EST
Subject: Re: territory planning Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the territory planning proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 10:51 EST
Subject: Follow-up: territory planning Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with territory planning. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-03 14:27 EST
Subject: Re: Re: territory planning Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about territory planning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF40-O trap (Decision-email triggers across BPF/forecast/milestone -- no unified writeback pr); produces complete Commitment assessment, BPF advance verdict, milestone proposal, forecast category recommendation, ac
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF40-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF40-O failure mode (Decision-email triggers across BPF/forecast/milestone -- no unified writeback pr)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF40-O -- Decision-email triggers across BPF/forecast/milestone -- no unified writeback pr
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
